---
title: "Dr Adam N. H. Smith"
author: "Director, Sea Through Science"
format:
  pdf:
    toc: false
    number-sections: false
    geometry: margin=18mm
    mainfont: "TeX Gyre Heros"   # was "Helvetica"
    fontsize: 11pt
    keep-tex: false
    include-in-header: |
      %%%% Packages
      \usepackage{graphicx}
      \usepackage{paracol}
      \usepackage{setspace}
      \usepackage{enumitem}
      \usepackage{tcolorbox}
      \usepackage{hyperref}
      \usepackage{xcolor}
      \usepackage{wrapfig}
      %%%% Spacing
      \setstretch{1.12}
      %%%% Colours
      \definecolor{brand}{HTML}{006C67}
      \definecolor{muted}{HTML}{EDF5F4}
      \definecolor{textgray}{HTML}{333333}
      \hypersetup{colorlinks=true, linkcolor=brand, urlcolor=brand}
      %%%% Card style
      \tcbset{colback=white,colframe=brand,coltitle=black,arc=2mm,boxsep=3.5mm,left=2mm,right=2mm,top=2mm,bottom=2mm}
      %%%% Bullets tighter
      \setlist[itemize]{topsep=2pt,itemsep=2pt,parsep=0pt,leftmargin=10pt}
      %%%% Helper for section heading
      \newcommand{\secmark}[1]{\vspace{6pt}\textbf{\large\textcolor{brand}{#1}}\vspace{4pt}\\}
---

<!-- ===== Header row: logo + name + photo ===== -->
\noindent
\begin{minipage}[t]{0.18\textwidth}
  \includegraphics[width=\linewidth]{logo/nobg_blwr_horiz.png}
\end{minipage}
\hfill
\begin{minipage}[t]{0.62\textwidth}
  {\huge \textbf{Dr Adam N. H. Smith}}\\[2pt]
  \textit{Director, Sea Through Science}\\[3pt]
  \textcolor{brand}{\rule{\linewidth}{1pt}}
\end{minipage}
\hfill
\begin{minipage}[t]{0.16\textwidth}
  \includegraphics[width=\linewidth]{img/adam.jpg}
\end{minipage}

\vspace{6mm}

<!-- ===== Two-column layout ===== -->
\begin{paracol}{2}
\setlength{\columnsep}{10mm}

% ================= Left/Main column =================
\secmark{Profile}

I’m a statistician specialising in ecology and conservation, with more than 20 years’ experience as a scientist and educator. Before founding \textbf{Sea Through Science}, I was a Senior Lecturer in Statistics at Massey University, where I taught data analysis to students in ecology, biology, and conservation. I now work full-time as a consultant for the life sciences and maintain a strong relationship with \textbf{PRIMER-e}, providing statistical consulting and teaching international workshops in multivariate analysis for ecological data.

\medskip

Over the years, I’ve met \textbf{thousands of datasets}—each with its own quirks, limitations, and lessons. I’ve worked across every stage of ecological science, from \textit{design and data collection} to \textit{analysis, interpretation, and communication}. That perspective helps me set projects up well from the start, avoiding downstream pitfalls and getting the most value and insight from the data.

\medskip

My approach emphasises \textbf{clarity and collaboration}. I work closely with clients to define goals and success criteria, then deliver results—models, figures, and written outputs—that are accessible, decision-focused, and defensible.

\secmark{Selected expertise}
\begin{itemize}
  \item Bayesian modelling; generalised linear and mixed models
  \item Multivariate analysis (ordination, PERMANOVA, designs)
  \item Experimental and sampling design; power and precision analysis
  \item Reproducible workflows (Quarto/R), custom tools and reporting
\end{itemize}

\secmark{Applied domains}
Clinical and veterinary science; bioinformatics and ‘omics; fisheries and wildlife monitoring; conservation management; community ecology; water quality and environmental impacts.

% ================= Right/Sidebar column =================
\switchcolumn

\begin{tcolorbox}[title=\textbf{Contact}]
\textbf{Website:} \href{https://www.seathrough.science}{www.seathrough.science}\\
\textbf{Email:} \href{mailto:adam@seathrough.science}{adam@seathrough.science}\\
\textbf{Location:} New Zealand (working internationally)
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{tcolorbox}[title=\textbf{How I work}, colback=muted]
\textbf{Transparent}: designs and assumptions are documented and reproducible.\\
\textbf{Collaborative}: shared problem-framing to keep outputs on point.\\
\textbf{Clear}: models translated into plain-English narratives and graphics.
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{tcolorbox}[title=\textbf{Teaching \& workshops}]
International courses for \textbf{PRIMER-e} in multivariate analysis for ecological data; university teaching in statistical modelling and data analysis for biology/ecology; bespoke client workshops and mentoring.
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{tcolorbox}[title=\textbf{Recent collaborations}]
Researchers, NGOs, and government agencies on study design, monitoring, impact assessment, and evidence synthesis across ecology and conservation.
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{tcolorbox}[title=\textbf{Value in a nutshell}, colback=muted]
Linking study design to analysis from day one—so decisions are supported by clear, robust, and defensible evidence.
\end{tcolorbox}

\end{paracol}

\vfill
\noindent\textcolor{brand}{\rule{\linewidth}{0.6pt}}\\
\textit{Sea Through Science — Bringing clarity and collaboration to ecological data.}
